test.observation: 123 tests passed (104 existing + 19 new delivery control tests)

lint.observation: No lint script in packages (not a failure)

nonBlockingIssues.description: createDeliveryRun accepts artifactId but never validates or uses it. The artifact existence is not verified before creating the run.
createPlanningArtifact requires ideaId but does not verify the idea exists or belongs to the company/project before creating the planning artifact.
WorkspaceLease entities are created when a delivery run starts but never have isActive set to false when the run completes, fails, or is cancelled. The lease remains 'active' indefinitely.
pauseDeliveryRun does not validate the current status before pausing. It allows pausing runs that are already completed, failed, or cancelled.
resumeDeliveryRun always sets status to 'running' regardless of what the status was before pause.
updateCompanyBudget uses autopilotUsedMinutes from params directly without validation. If called with a negative value, it would reduce used minutes below zero.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the pattern for entity existence validation. The current implementation does not validate that ideaId/artifactId exist before creating dependent entities. While this is non-blocking for delivery-control, it is a pattern that should be addressed across the codebase.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: createDeliveryRun and createPlanningArtifact both accept foreign key IDs without existence validation. The same pattern likely exists in other entity creation helpers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The autopilot plugin's entity helpers filter by companyId AFTER retrieving entities from the entity store, rather than relying on the store's scopeId filtering.
sharedStateObservations.implication: Other features should follow the same pattern of applying companyId filters in application code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Automation tier (supervised/semiauto/fullauto) determines approvalMode only at PlanningArtifact creation time. Changes to automation tier after artifact creation do not retroactively update existing artifacts.
sharedStateObservations.implication: This is likely intentional but should be documented in the Product Program or feature spec.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Budget cap auto-pause only triggers on createDeliveryRun, not on createPlanningArtifact. Ideas can still produce planning artifacts even when budget is exceeded.
sharedStateObservations.implication: The budget pause is at the 'execution' level (delivery runs) not at the 'planning' level. This is a design decision that may surprise users.
